name.es: Pain au levain (pan de masa madre francés)

description.en: Flour, water, salt and a lively natural levain, nothing else. A long, cool bulk rise builds a tangy, complex loaf with a thin, crackling crust and a wide-open, glossy crumb.
description.de: Mehl, Wasser, Salz und ein lebendiger Natursauerteig, mehr nicht. Eine lange, kühle Stockgare erzeugt ein würzig-komplexes Brot mit dünner, krachender Kruste und weitporiger, glänzender Krume.
description.es: Harina, agua, sal y un levain natural bien activo, nada más. Una fermentación en bloque larga y fresca produce una hogaza ácida y compleja, de corteza fina y crujiente y miga abierta y brillante.
description.fr: Farine, eau, sel et un levain naturel bien vivant, rien de plus. Un long pointage au frais donne un pain complexe et acidulé, à la croûte fine et craquante et à la mie grande ouverte et brillante.

origin.note: The pre-industrial way of leavening French bread, kept alive by artisan bakers even as commercial yeast took over most everyday loaves.

keeping.note: The natural acidity of the levain slows staling well beyond a yeasted white loaf.

method.title: Refresh the levain
method.summary: Feed the liquid levain 5-7 hours ahead so it's active, bubbly and roughly doubled.

method.title: Autolyse
method.summary: Mix the flour and water and rest 40-60 minutes before adding anything else.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Work in the levain and knead until strong and elastic, adding the salt partway through.

method.title: Bulk with folds
method.summary: Ferment with a few gentle folds, then retard in the fridge overnight for depth of flavor.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Shape into one large round and set it into a well-floured banneton.

method.title: Proof
method.summary: Let rise until puffy and light, about an hour once out of the cold.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Score and bake hot with steam, then lower the heat to bake all the way through.

source.adaptation: Combined the site's 'intermediate' 67%-hydration formula with the levain's own flour/water folded into the totals, giving 72% by Panarium's convention.
